mnemonics: Frames Are Meaning's Narrative: Every Meaning Needs A Relational Story (Frames, Annotated, Meaning, Narrative, Evokes, Meaning, Argument, Roles, Semantics)
keyPoints: FrameNet is a lexical database based on Frame Semantics.
It organizes vocabulary around conceptual structures called 'frames'.
Each frame contains frame elements (roles) that participants and props play in the scenario.
commonMistakes: Confusing FrameNet with WordNet (FrameNet is based on scenarios/events, WordNet on synonyms/synsets).
Assuming a word belongs to only one frame (many words, especially verbs, can evoke multiple frames).
